Title: Deploy a Minecraft-specific MemorySmith wiki
Description:
The agent currently has no live MemorySmith wiki to query for Minecraft-specific knowledge (item-registry pages, blueprints, biome data). The codebase at http://localhost:6868 is the MemorySmith instance for the MemorySmith.Agent *codebase* notes — not a game-content knowledge base.

The MemorySmith app (d:\@Repos\MemorySmith) needs to be deployed as a second instance dedicated to Minecraft agent knowledge. The agent's RestMemoryGateway (Agent:Memory:BaseUrl) should point at this Minecraft wiki.

Implementation steps:
1. Deploy a second MemorySmith.App instance (e.g. http://localhost:7000 or a hosted URL).
   - Configure a separate database/data folder so Minecraft content is isolated.
2. Seed it with the checked-in wiki pages from MemorySmith.Agent/Data/Pages/ (item-registry/*, blueprints/*).
   - Script: iterate Data/Pages and POST each file to /api/pages on the new instance.
3. Update Agent:Memory:BaseUrl in WebUI.Blazor/appsettings.json to point at the Minecraft wiki.
4. Verify: 'gather wood' should resolve via live HTTP page lookup instead of local file fallback.
5. (Stretch) Auto-seed script so new item-registry and blueprint pages added locally are pushed to the wiki on startup.

Connected issues:
- Without a live wiki, Search calls always return empty results — the LLM and memory tools can't learn from Minecraft-specific context.
- Blueprint creation ('build a house') depends on the wiki for the blueprint page content.
- Item variant lookup depends on the wiki for per-item source-blocks data.

Blocking until resolved:
- Blueprint building in production runs.
- SearchMemory tool returning useful context for planning.
